title: Operations

Operations

Operations

Operations are ProcScript modules that are analogous to methods in other programming languages; they perform specific actions and can be activated by other ProcScript modules, and even by non-Uniface components.

Operations are defined in dedicated code containers—the Script container of components, and the Collection Operations and Occurrence Operations containers of modeled entities.

An operation is declared using an operation...end declaration; for example:

operation do_it
 ; <Your code here>
end

It can be called using the activate ProcScript statement.

In dynamic server pages, it is possible to define web operations, which can be called and executed in the client browser. These are declared using the weboperation declaration, but implemented in JavaScript (enclosed between the javascript and endjavascript ProcScript commands). For example:

weboperation client_do_it
javascript
 <Your JavaScript here>
endjavascript
end

Operations can be modified using the public or partner modifier. Public operations can be invoked from other components. Partner operations can only be invoked from the component in which they are defined. If no modifier is used, operations are considered public operations.

Public operations are exposed to other components via the component's signature which is its interface description. The signature defines the component name, its operations and their sequence, and the parameters for each operation, including their data type and direction. The interface shields the actual implementation of the components, allowing components of different origin to communicate through their respective interfaces.

Note: It is also possible to define the signatures of third-party components, making it possible to integrate them into Uniface applications. For more information, see Integrating with Other Technologies and Signatures.

The exec operation is a component's default operation. The exec operation and the accept and quit triggers are exposed in the component signature and can be invoked using the activate statement.

Operation Parameters

Operations exchange information via input and output parameters. Parameters can be:

  • Fields and component variables, both of which are local to a component instance
  • Named parameters that are local to an operation
  • Entities
  • Occurrence handles
  • Structs
  • XML streams, defined by a DTD
Related Links
  • Public and Partner Operations

    Operations can be modified using the public or partner modifier. Public operations can be invoked from other components. Partner operations can only be invoked from the component in which they are defined.

  • Predefined Operations

    Predefined operations are operations and triggers that Uniface executes in specific circumstances. If declared, these ProcScript modules are exposed in the component signature.

  • operation

    Declare an operation ProcScript module.

  • operation exec

    Default component operation, which is invoked by the activate statement.

  • operation accept

    Defines the behavior that should occur in response to an Accept request. If an Accept trigger is defined, it is exposed as the accept operation in the component signature.

  • operation quit

    Defines the processing that should occur during a Quit request. If a quit trigger is defined in a component, it is exposed as the quit operation in the component signature.

  • activate

    Calls an operation on a component instance.
